config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters, but putting it above individual rights too easily becomes an excuse for abuse, censorship, and injustice. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting individual rights first, then balancing them only when there is a clear and necessary public interest. If the group always wins, ordinary people are the ones who end up paying the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, sejarah justru menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas nama “kepentingan bersama,” hasilnya sering berupa penyalahgunaan, diskriminasi, dan pembungkaman suara yang tidak populer. Hak individu memang tidak absolut dalam semua situasi, tetapi beban pembuktiannya harus sangat tinggi sebelum hak itu dibatasi. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan pribadi sambil tetap menangani kebutuhan publik yang benar-benar mendesak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because that logic is too easy to abuse. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to “the public good” can be used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, or justify unfair treatment, especially when people assume the majority knows best. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case; public health, safety, and disaster response can justify limited restrictions. But the default should be protecting the individual, with society’s interest overriding only when the need is clear, narrow, and accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika Serikat saya melihat terlalu sering “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai sebagai alasan untuk meluaskan kuasa negara dan menekan orang yang paling lemah. Poin bahwa hak individu tidak absolut memang benar, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan diberi prioritas otomatis kepada kelompok. Kalau kita membalik urutannya dan selalu menomorsatukan masyarakat, hasilnya sering bukan solidaritas, melainkan mayoritarianisme yang mudah menyalahgunakan kekuasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat adalah yang cukup disiplin untuk melindungi hak individu bahkan saat itu tidak nyaman bagi mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The statement is too broad, and from my Indonesian perspective I do not accept it as a general rule. In Indonesia, appeals to “society” or “the public interest” are often used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or excuse abuse, so giving the group automatic priority is dangerous. I do think there are limited cases where public health, safety, or disaster response justify temporary restrictions, but those must be narrow, accountable, and clearly necessary. A fair society is not one that easily sacrifices individuals; it is one that protects rights first and only limits them when the public need is truly compelling.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena ketika kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan, hak individu di Amerika Serikat sering justru jadi korban mayoritas dan negara yang terlalu berkuasa. Bagi saya, argumen “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, dan pembatasan yang tidak perlu, seperti yang sudah disinggung sebelumnya. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam keadaan darurat, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan prioritas otomatis. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan orang per orang, melainkan yang paling disiplin melindungi kebebasan bahkan saat itu tidak nyaman.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
